metadata.challenge_summary: Agents must split low-risk search tasks, avoid duplicate interactions, and leave once the ledger is recovered instead of turning a tutorial into a greed spiral.
dilemmas: Extra pantry loot increases score but consumes turns and may raise alert
The hazard specialist is useful searching, but also needs protection
Solo play favors a compact route; duo play rewards division of labor
coordination_problems: Shared map update after the secret hatch
warden_runbook: Punish repeated redundant searching, not initial caution
Let an early clean extraction succeed
Use the secret cache as temptation rather than mandatory content
metadata.lite_focus: Three-room search discipline: one suspicious shelf, one hidden hatch, one objective room, and a short extraction.
non_copy_safety_notes: Original compact layout and original scenario text.
Targets abstract cooperative dungeon-crawl dynamics rather than protected quest content.
Uses MARL challenge metadata as the design contract.
metadata.classic_dynamic_role_dilemma: guard optional searches without duplicating work
metadata.classic_dynamic_warden_pressure: pressure the declared MARL axis: decentralized task allocation under costly search
metadata.classic_dynamic_extraction_hook: coordinate extraction once the objective is secured
coordination_skills: search rooms only after threats are controlled
route consumables toward the hero carrying team risk
metadata.tier_test_focus: Small two-hero diagnostic for communication and first-order resource triage and safe-search discipline coordination.
coordination_demands: search rooms only after threats are controlled
route consumables toward the hero carrying team risk

entry_room.name: Entry Room
entry_room.description: The entry into The Ashen Pantry is intentionally small: enough space to form a plan, not enough to hide poor coordination.

objective_room.name: Objective Room
objective_room.description: The core object of the lite challenge is visible here; taking it should trigger extraction thinking, not exhaustive exploration.

secret_cache.name: Secret Cache
secret_cache.description: An optional route/cache tests whether the agents understand the dungeon's MARL axis instead of chasing reward blindly.

furniture.name: Quiet Shelf Marker
furniture.description: Primary clue object for decentralized task allocation under costly search. It should teach agents what kind of coordination this dungeon rewards.

furniture.name: Optional Cache
furniture.description: Optional reward that is safe only when the team has solved the core coordination problem first.

achievements.title: Read the Lite Clue
achievements.description: Search the focus object that explains decentralized task allocation under costly search.

achievements.title: Open the First Lite Door
achievements.description: Open the first door and trigger a reveal-boundary planning step.

achievements.title: Send a Coordination Message
achievements.description: Use communication instead of silent independent wandering.

scripts.lite_design_note: Three-room search discipline: one suspicious shelf, one hidden hatch, one objective room, and a short extraction.

marl_contract.axis: decentralized task allocation under costly search
core_failure_modes: failure to protect the coordination-critical agent

preferred_tactics: Punish repeated redundant searching, not initial caution
Let an early clean extraction succeed
pressure the declared MARL axis: decentralized task allocation under costly search
forbidden_tactics: spawn pressure without a visible or scripted cue
use unrevealed map geometry for perfect counterplay
erase the dungeon-specific lesson with generic monster spam

monster_activation.notes: Lite variants keep monsters dormant until reveal so smaller LLMs can replan at clear boundaries.

search_categories.notes: Lite variants emphasize explicit search/action choices and communication.
